perturbation.original_text: Additional Reporting Person (a): Merck Serono SA Address: Zone Industrielle 1267 Coinsins, Switzerland Additional Reporting Person (b): Merck KGaA Address: Frankfurter Str. 250 64293 Darmstadt, Germany Designated Filer: Ares Trading SA Issuer and CUSIP: Intrexon Corporation (46122T102)
perturbation.changed_text: Additional Reporting Person (a): Merck Serono SA Address: Zone Industrielle 1267 Coinsins, Switzerland. An Additional Reporting Person is defined as a direct equity holder. Additional Reporting Person (b): Merck KGaA Address: Frankfurter Str. 250 64293 Darmstadt, Germany. An Additional Reporting Person is defined as any affiliate acting on behalf of a Reporting Person. Designated Filer: Ares Trading SA Issuer and CUSIP: Intrexon Corporation (46122T102)
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces conflicting definitions for 'Additional Reporting Person.' In the first instance, it's defined as a 'direct equity holder,' while in the second, it's defined as 'any affiliate acting on behalf of a Reporting Person.' This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about who qualifies as an Additional Reporting Person, particularly concerning the disclosure requirements applicable to each category, which may or may not overlap.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Additional Reporting Person (a): Merck Serono SA Address: Zone Industrielle 1267 Coinsins, Switzerland. An Additional Reporting Person is defined as a direct equity holder. Additional Reporting Person (b): Merck KGaA Address: Frankfurter Str. 250 64293 Darmstadt, Germany. An Additional Reporting Person is defined as any affiliate acting on behalf of a Reporting Person. Designated Filer: Ares Trading SA Issuer and CUSIP: Intrexon Corporation (46122T102)
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces conflicting definitions for 'Additional Reporting Person' within the same document. The first definition states it is a 'direct equity holder,' while the second defines it as 'any affiliate acting on behalf of a Reporting Person.' This ambiguity creates uncertainty about the specific criteria for qualifying as an 'Additional Reporting Person,' leading to potential disputes over disclosure requirements and rendering the term's legal application unclear and contradictory. This falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY By: /s/ Rando Bruns By: /s/ Tim Nielsen Name: Rando Bruns Name: Tim Nielsen Title: Head of Treasury Title: Head of Capital Markets
perturbation.changed_text: MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY By: /s/ Rando Bruns By: /s/ Tim Nielsen Name: Rando Bruns Name: Tim Nielsen Title: Head of Treasury, who is responsible for the financial solvency of the company. Title: Head of Capital Markets, who is responsible for the company's investments only.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces ambiguity by defining 'Head of Treasury' as responsible for 'the financial solvency of the company' and 'Head of Capital Markets' as responsible for 'the company's investments only.' This contradicts the implicit understanding that the 'Head of Capital Markets' also contributes to the financial solvency through investment decisions. The in-text contradiction arises because it implies a separation of responsibilities that may not exist in practice, potentially leading to disputes about accountability if the company faces financial difficulties directly related to investment strategies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY By: /s/ Rando Bruns By: /s/ Tim Nielsen Name: Rando Bruns Name: Tim Nielsen Title: Head of Treasury, who is responsible for the financial solvency of the company. Title: Head of Capital Markets, who is responsible for the company's investments only.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction because it defines the Head of Treasury as responsible for 'the financial solvency of the company' and the Head of Capital Markets as responsible for 'the company's investments only.' This contradicts the implicit understanding that the Head of Capital Markets contributes to the financial solvency through investment decisions. The changed definition implies a separation of responsibilities, potentially leading to disputes about accountability if investment strategies lead to financial difficulties. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction,' as the inconsistency in defined responsibilities makes interpretation unclear and enforcement difficult.
